snp4.4:1.1: “I see the pure, the supreme, free from disease.
snp4.4:1.2: It’s in connection with what’s seen that a person’s purity is.”
snp4.4:1.4: & remaining focused on purity, one falls back on that knowledge.
snp4.4:2.1: If it’s in connection with what is seen that a person’s purity is,
snp4.4:2.2: or if stress is abandoned in connection with knowledge,
snp4.4:2.3: then a person with acquisitions is purified in connection with something else,
snp4.4:2.4: for his view betrays that in the way he asserts it.
snp4.4:3.1:
No brahman says purity comes in connection with anything else.
Unsmeared with regard
snp4.4:3.2: to what’s seen, heard, sensed, habits or practices,
snp4.4:3.4: not creating anything here, he’s let go of what he’d embraced.
snp4.4:4.1: Abandoning what’s first, they depend on what’s next.
snp4.4:4.2: Following perturbation, they don’t cross over the bond.
snp4.4:4.4: —like a monkey releasing a branch to seize at another—
snp4.4:5.1: a person undertaking practices on his own,
snp4.4:5.2: goes high & low, latched onto perception.
snp4.4:5.3: But having clearly known through vedas, having encountered the Dhamma,
snp4.4:5.4: one deeply discerning doesn’t go high & low.
snp4.4:6.1: He’s enemy-free with regard to all things
snp4.4:6.4: By whom, with what, should he be pigeonholed here in the world? —one who has seen in this way, who goes around open.
snp4.4:7.3: Untying the tied-up knot of grasping,
snp4.4:7.4: they don’t form a desire anywhere in the world.
snp4.4:8.2: has nothing that —on knowing or seeing— he’s grasped.
snp4.4:8.3: Unimpassionate for passion, not impassioned for dis-,
snp4.4:8.4: he has nothing here that he’s grasped as supreme.
